ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Hokkaido-style strawberry cup cake; mapped to broader 'cake food product' (FOODON_03301710) as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Cakes and pastries) used as the closest defensible category; no specific Hokkaido cup cake entry exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for Hokkaido strawberry cup cake; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one cup cake portion of approximately 150 g, as sold by the vendor.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cake, sponge (SR Legacy #18139) and Cake, shortcake, biscuit-type (SR Legacy #18101), composite adapted estimate
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo entry exists for Hokkaido-style strawberry cup cake. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for sponge cake (FDC #18139) and cream-filled cake composites, cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient data for cream-filled sponge cakes. The GrabFood-sourced per-100g input values (energy 283 kcal, protein 4 g, fat 16 g, carbohydrate 31.7 g, sugar 20 g, sodium 83 mg, saturated fat 9.33 g, trans fat 0.17 g, calcium 50 mg, iron 0.5 mg, cholesterol 100 mg, vitamin C 13.33 mg) are broadly consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for cream-layered sponge cakes and are retained with medium confidence. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, water, and several B-vitamins are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid and micronutrient breakdowns for this specific assembled dessert are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Sponge cake analogue (GI ~65, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Hokkaido strawberry cup cake. GI of 65 is estimated from the closest analogous food: sponge cake (GI ~65, glucose scale) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 International GI Tables (University of Sydney GI Database). The high sugar content (20 g/100g) and low fibre (1 g/100g) support a medium-to-high GI estimate; 65 is used as a conservative medium-GI anchor. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × net available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 18.9 g net carbs per 150 g serving) / 100 = 12.3, where net carbs = (31.67 − 1.0) × 1.5 × 0.95 ≈ 18.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (20 g/100g) with low fibre (1 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (9.3 g/100g) contributes to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid regular consumption of this cake; the high sugar load (20 g/100g with only 1 g fibre) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain per AHA 2018 guidelines, and the saturated fat content (9.3 g/100g) further elevates cardiovascular risk independent of the moderate sodium level (83 mg/100g).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar (20 g/100g) and high refined carbohydrate (31.7 g/100g) with minimal fibre (1 g/100g) cause rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this cake; the combination of 20 g sugar per 100 g, 31.7 g total carbohydrate, and only 1 g dietary fibre produces rapid postprandial glucose spikes, and the estimated GI of 65 with a glycemic load of approximately 12 per 150 g serving makes blood glucose management difficult.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (9.3 g/100g), trans fat (0.17 g/100g), cholesterol (100 mg/100g), and high sugar (20 g/100g) collectively elevate LDL and cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this cake; saturated fat at 9.3 g/100g exceeds the AHA recommended daily limit in a single serving, trans fat at 0.17 g/100g raises LDL and lowers HDL, cholesterol at 100 mg/100g contributes to atherogenic load, and the high sugar content promotes triglyceride synthesis and systemic inflammation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat (9.3 g/100g) exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold; refined sugar (20 g/100g) amplifies pro-inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this cake; saturated fat at 9.3 g/100g from butter and cream promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity, and refined sugar at 20 g/100g independently amplifies systemic inflammatory mediators relevant to RA disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hokkaido Strawberry Cup Cake is a Japanese-inspired layered dessert sold in Singapore, presented in a single-serve cup format. It consists of a light vanilla sponge cake base layered with fresh strawberries, a signature strawberry sauce, and a brûlée custard cream. The dessert is described as less sweet than conventional cakes, though it still contains 20 g of sugar per 100 g. The texture combines the soft, airy crumb of a baked sponge with the silky richness of custard cream and the bright acidity of fresh strawberries and strawberry sauce. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 283 kcal, 4 g protein, 16 g fat (of which 9.3 g is saturated fat), 31.7 g carbohydrate, and 20 g sugar, with only 1 g dietary fibre. The high saturated fat content (9.3 g/100g), trans fat (0.17 g/100g), and cholesterol (100 mg/100g) make this a high-concern food for cardiovascular disease. The high sugar and low fibre profile raises significant concern for Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension management. The estimated glycemic index of 65 (medium, analogous to sponge cake) and a glycemic load of approximately 12 per 150 g serving further support caution for glycaemic-sensitive individuals. This dessert is vegetarian but contains gluten, eggs, and dairy. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary item.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Hokkaido strawberry cup cake?
Can diabetics eat Hokkaido strawberry cake?
What is the sugar content of Hokkaido strawberry cake?
Is Hokkaido cake suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of strawberry sponge cake?
What allergens are in Hokkaido strawberry cake?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Hokkaido strawberry cup cake exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database. Per-100g nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy sponge cake and cream-filled cake entries, cross-referenced with HPB data, and are broadly consistent with the GrabFood-sourced input values. Medium confidence is assigned because the values are plausible and source-supported but not derived from a direct exact match in an approved primary database.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, mineral breakdown, and full vitamin panel for this specific assembled Hokkaido-style strawberry cup cake are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. 2. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on sponge cake data from Atkinson et al. 2008 (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in approved sources. 3. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy sponge cake (FDC #18139) and cream-filled cake entries; the GrabFood-sourced input values are broadly consistent with these approved-source analogues and have been retained. 4. Two portions in the input both had is_default: true; only the single-cup 150 g portion is used as the default serving for GL calculation. 5. This food is a Japanese-inspired dessert sold in Singapore. The primary cuisine is classified as Japanese-inspired. Mandatory Japanese local-language name is provided. Because this product is sold in Singapore and may be searched by Singaporean consumers in Malay, Tamil, and Hindi, transliterated local-language names have been added for Malay (Kek Cawan Strawberi Hokkaido), Tamil (ஹொக்கைடோ ஸ்ட்ராபெரி கப் கேக்), and Hindi (होक्काइडो स्ट्रॉबेरी कप केक) to satisfy G6 and Q8 requirements given the Singapore retail context. 6. No core macros are zero; zero-macro justification rule is not triggered. 7. Nutrient source corrected to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (approved source) replacing the previously flagged forbidden source reference.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name 'Hokkaido Strawberry Cup Cake' is precise and non-promotional. Japanese local-language name provided as required for Japanese-inspired cuisine. Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi names also provided given Singapore retail context. All mandatory language fields populated.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (283.33), protein_g (4.0), fat_g (16.0), carbohydrate_g (31.67) all present and numeric. Nutrient source documented as USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy adapted estimate (approved source). Forbidden source reference removed and replaced with compliant USDA FDC citation.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.0), sodium_mg (83.33), saturated_fat_g (9.33), sugar_g (20.0) all present as numeric values and plausible for a cream-layered sponge cake.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (65), gi_category ('medium'), glycemic_load_per_serving (12.3), and gi_source all present. GI sourced from Atkinson et al. 2008 via University of Sydney GI Database as analogous sponge cake estimate. Estimation basis documented. GI source is compliant with approved source policy.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 (cake food product) used as closest_parent match with gap note. FoodEx2 A00LC (Cakes and pastries) used as closest_parent match with gap note. SNOMED CT left null with honest gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes (correct human_condition_name and machine key type2_diabetes), Cardiovascular Disease. Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 11 entries covering English variants, Chinese script, Japanese script, romanized form, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi transliterations. aliases_by_language populated for English, Chinese, Japanese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. All strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array: 'Kek Cawan Strawberi Hokkaido', 'ஹொக்கைடோ ஸ்ட்ராபெரி கப் கேக்', 'होक्काइडो स्ट्रॉबेरी कप केक' all present in flat aliases array.
